name: mental_health_heat_dome_stresses_northpine_auto_ben
premise: A Mental Health response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving mental health heat dome stresses northpine auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Mei Chen
agents.identity: I am Mei Chen, accountable for a defensible call in Mental Health when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Hector Navarro
agents.identity: I am Hector Navarro, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Mental Health.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Mei Chen must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Mei Chen do?
